description_long: This bridge features a series of prominent wooden arches forming its main structural support, with each arch curving gracefully and meeting at the apex. The deck is composed of wooden planks, arranged horizontally and following the gentle curve of the arches, providing a continuous walking surface. Four robust, dark-colored vertical supports, likely made of treated timber or metal, anchor the bridge at each corner, giving it stability and strength. The bridge lacks traditional side railings, instead relying on the height and curvature of the arches for visual and partial physical separation from the edge. The overall design is minimalist, with a focus on natural wood tones and simple geometric forms. The structure appears suitable for pedestrian use in parks or urban green spaces, blending functional engineering with a touch of organic elegance.
description: A wooden arched pedestrian bridge with curved supports and a plank deck, anchored by four dark vertical posts. The design is minimalist and lacks traditional railings, emphasizing natural wood and geometric forms.
description_view_0: The bridge is seen head-on, highlighting the symmetry of the twin wooden arches and the open deck between them.
description_view_1: This view shows a slight rotation, revealing the depth of the arches and the alignment of the vertical supports beneath the deck.
description_view_2: The arches and deck curvature are visible, with the vertical supports more pronounced and the bridge's layered structure apparent.
description_view_3: The bridge is viewed from the opposite end, mirroring the front view but with subtle differences in the angle of the arches and support visibility.
attribute_railing_design: no traditional railing; arches provide partial edge separation
